Ad Text:
Senior Clinical Project Manager

Locations: Australia & New Zealand

The Role

At IQVIA, we accelerate innovation to improve patient outcomes worldwide. As a Senior Clinical Project Manager, you will independently lead and deliver medium to large, multi‑regional clinical studies, partnering closely with clinical teams and customers to bring new therapies to market faster. You will hold end‑to‑end responsibility for study delivery in line with contractual requirements and SOPs, acting as the primary customer contact and leading cross‑functional teams to achieve milestones on time, within budget and to the highest quality standards, while driving strong financial and customer outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Lead and contribute to bid defence activities in partnership with Business Development for multi‑service, multi‑country, and/or multi‑regional studies
Translate project strategy into clear, executable operational plans
Develop and maintain integrated study management plans with the core project team
Provide strategic leadership and oversight to ensure delivery of clinical studies in line with scope, timelines, quality, and cost expectations
Lead, motivate and manage cross‑functional project teams to achieve agreed objectives and milestones
Act as the primary point of contact for customers, owning and strengthening key client relationships
Monitor progress against contractual obligations and proactively communicate project status, risks, and solutions to internal and external stakeholders
Manage project risks, issues, and contingencies, leading problem‑solving and resolution efforts
Ensure project quality by identifying risks, responding to issues, and implementing corrective and preventive action plans
Drive financial success through forecasting, revenue acceleration opportunities and change control management
Identify changes in scope and manage the change control process effectively
Capture and share lessons learned and best practices to support continuous improvement
Support staff development through mentoring, coaching and performance feedback
Act as a change advocate, adopting and promoting corporate initiatives

Essential Qualifications & Experience

Bachelor’s degree in Life Sciences or a related field
Minimum 10 years of clinical research experience, including at least 6 years in clinical project management
Minimum 5 years’ experience delivering healthy volunteer (Phase I) studies
Proven experience managing medium to large, multi‑regional, or full‑service clinical trials
Strong working knowledge of ICH‑GCP and applicable local regulatory requirements

Key Skills & Competencies

Strong understanding of clinical trial conduct, protocols, and therapeutic areas
Ability to lead through others, drive results, manage performance and work effectively across functions and geographies
Excellent written, verbal and presentation skills; strong negotiation and customer management capability
Proactive, analytical, and solutions‑focused approach
Demonstrated ability to manage competing priorities and complex timelines
Experience managing project financials, forecasts, and contractual obligations
High attention to detail, continuous improvement mindset and learning agility
Proficiency with MS Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
Strong relationship‑building skills with clients, peers, and stakeholders

Why IQVIA?

IQVIA is a global leader in clinical research, commercial insights, and healthcare intelligence. We offer:

Competitive salary package with 2 additional annual leave days per year
Flexibility to work at home and in 1 of 3 IQVIA offices (Hybrid for metro NSW, metro VIC, Auckland NZ) OR 100% remote if in QLD, WA, SA, TAS, regional NSW/VIC and NZ
Exposure to innovative and impactful clinical research
Opportunities to lead complex, global studies
A collaborative, high‑performance culture
Career development, flexibility and support across Australia and New Zealand

Own the study. Lead the science. Make a global impact

Apply today with you resume and a member of the Talent Acquisition team will be in contact via email

Referrals welcome

IQVIA is a leading global provider of clinical research services, commercial insights and healthcare intelligence to the life sciences and healthcare industries. We create intelligent connections to accelerate the development and commercialization of innovative medical treatments to help improve patient outcomes and population health worldwide. Learn more at https://jobs.iqvia.com

IQVIA is committed to integrity in our hiring process and maintains a zero tolerance policy for candidate fraud. All information and credentials submitted in your application must be truthful and complete. Any false statements, misrepresentations, or material omissions during the recruitment process will result in immediate disqualification of your application, or termination of employment if discovered later, in accordance with applicable law. We appreciate your honesty and professionalism.

Randstad is seeking a high-achieving Senior Compliance Analyst with a meticulous eye for detail and a proven track record of excellence. This role is designed for a relationship-builder who thrives in a collaborative environment and is eager to tackle complex challenges within the Federal Government sector.

About your new role

As a Senior Analyst, Compliance, you will play a pivotal role in supporting the Director with the performance and management of day-to-day Compliance activities. This multifaceted position balances system administration such as user access and personal trading platforms with the enforcement of internal policies regarding conflicts of interest and incident management. Additionally, you will maintain external oversight by reviewing investment manager attestations to ensure strict adherence to mandates and regulatory requirements.

What you will need to exceed

An industry-relevant undergraduate degree in Business, Commerce, Accounting, or Law.
Ability to liaise with stakeholders at all levels and build effective relationships.
Proven experience in a Line 2 compliance, governance, or risk role, preferably within financial services.
A methodical and accurate approach with a high attention to detail.
Ability to work autonomously while ensuring all deadlines are met.

Must have

An industry-relevant undergraduate degree in business, commerce, accounting, or law

Desirable

Experience in working for government

Next steps

If you are a compliance expert with a commitment to inclusivity, collaboration, and transformative change, we invite you to apply and be part of this inspiring journey.

At Randstad, we are passionate about providing equal employment opportunities and embracing diversity to the benefit of all. We actively encourage applications from any background.

The Opportunity
Our client is seeking an experienced Commercial Manager to join a private equity-backed Australian business entering its next phase of growth.
Operating nationally, this organisation is becoming and icon of the gaming and entertainment space.  With a strong recurring revenue model, loyal customer base, and significant network effects, the organisation is well positioned for expansion.
  
The Role
You will be the most senior finance leader within the business and a key member of the executive leadership team. You will partner directly with the Executive Director, divisional leaders and board, providing commercial insight, building robust reporting frameworks, and helping drive EBITDA growth.  The responsibilities of your role will include but not be limited to the following.

Lead the full annual budgeting process, including divisional engagement and executive alignment
Build and maintain a 3–5 year financial model aligned to strategic growth initiatives
Translate strategic initiatives into financial outcomes, KPIs and measurable performance targets
Conduct detailed margin and contribution analysis across products, venues, and event categories
Analyse venue and partner economics to optimise pricing and incentives
Support evaluation of growth initiatives, new products, and potential acquisitions
Identify and drive EBITDA improvement opportunities
Act as a trusted commercial advisor to the Executive Director and leadership team
Challenge divisional leaders constructively on financial performance and strategy
Support pricing strategy, incentive design and margin optimisation
Improve financial accountability and commercial literacy across the organisation
Provide insight-led analysis to inform key strategic decisions
Design and implement a structured monthly management reporting framework
Deliver board-ready reporting aligned to private equity governance standards
Develop KPI dashboards and performance commentary for executive and board stakeholders
Implement rolling forecasting discipline and forward-looking financial analysis
Improve financial visibility across cashflow, margins, contractor economics and revenue drivers
Lead and structure the finance function as the business scales
Establish clear separation across commercial finance, accounting and transactional processing
Improve efficiency through process improvement and automation
Strengthen financial controls and governance frameworks
Reduce key-person dependency and introduce scalable finance processes  
The Candidate
With experience working within a PE environment, you are a commercially minded finance leader who thrives in entrepreneurial environments and enjoys building structure and insight in growing businesses. You combine strong analytical capability with the ability to influence senior stakeholders and translate financial data into clear commercial decisions.

8+ years’ experience in commercial finance, FP&A, or finance business partnering
Experience operating in multi-site businesses 
Proven ownership of budgeting, forecasting and financial modelling
Experience building management reporting frameworks from the ground up
Advanced Excel and strong financial modelling capability
Experience improving finance structures in lean or fast-growing businesses
To Apply 
To apply online, please click on the appropriate link below or contact David Petrou at dpetrou@sharpandcarter.com.au
For other positions advertised by Sharp & Carter please go to www.sharpandcarter.com.au

At Acquire Intelligence, our mission is to help business work smarter.

We bring together the best people, efficient processes, and automation to help companies grow faster and operate with confidence.

Our purpose is to simplify how businesses work, improve performance, and lead meaningful change across the globe.

If you’re passionate about making a real impact through innovation and collaboration, Acquire Intelligence is the place to grow your career.

Advance Your Career - Join us today and be part of a team where your work makes an impact!




WHY JOIN THE A-TEAM?

Come for a career, stay for the fun!

Enjoy a weekly pay run – TGI Thursday’s

Grow your career - yes, we love to promote internally

LK Group Discount - including Hommey, Daniel's Donuts, NBL and more

Do meaningful work and collaborate with the best




A SNAPSHOT OF YOUR ROLE

This role is crucial for ensuring a smooth and efficient transition for our clients, establishing strong relationships from the outset, and setting them up for long-term success with our solutions.

Your day-to-day activities may include the following:

Lead Client Onboarding Projects: Manage the end-to-end client onboarding lifecycle, from initial contract signing to successful platform implementation and handoff to account management.

Develop and Execute Onboarding Plans: Create detailed project plans, timelines, and communication strategies for each new client, aligning with their specific needs and our internal processes.

Cross-Functional Coordination: Act as the primary point of contact for new clients during onboarding, coordinating closely with internal teams including sales, product, engineering, support, and legal to ensure all aspects of onboarding are addressed.

Stakeholder Communication: Provide regular updates to clients and internal stakeholders on project progress, risks, and milestones. Proactively identify and address potential roadblocks.

Documentation and Training: Ensure comprehensive documentation of client configurations, requirements, and best practices. Facilitate client training sessions on Acquire Intelligence Inc. products and services.

Process Improvement: Continuously evaluate and refine onboarding processes to enhance efficiency, client satisfaction, and scalability. Identify and implement best practices for client success.

Risk Management: Identify potential risks to project success and develop mitigation strategies.

Relationship Building: Foster strong, positive relationships with new clients, acting as a trusted advisor throughout the onboarding phase.




A BIT ABOUT YOU

Preferable Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Project Management, or a related field. PMP or CSM certification is a plus.

3+ years of experience in project management, client onboarding, implementation, or a similar client-facing role, preferably within a BPO or SaaS company

Flexibility around work schedule for project assignments as required (this may include shifting working hours to align with other regions)

Proven ability to manage complex projects with multiple stakeholders, deadlines, and dependencies. Strong understanding of project management methodologies.

Exceptional verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to articulate technical concepts to non-technical audiences and effectively manage client expectations.

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a proactive approach to identifying and resolving issues.

Ability to quickly understand and articulate technical product features and integrate them into client solutions. Experience with CRM and project management software (e.g., Salesforce, Asana, Jira) is a plus.

A genuine passion for client success and a commitment to delivering an outstanding client experience.

Excellent organizational skills with the ability to prioritize tasks and manage multiple projects simultaneously in a fast-paced environment.




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

Projects delivered on time, within scope, and to agreed quality standards.

Strong, positive relationships maintained with clients and stakeholders.

Clear communication and collaboration across technical and business teams.

Continuous improvement initiatives implemented to enhance service quality.

High customer satisfaction and consistent achievement of service targets.




WHAT WE VALUE

We’re proud of our diverse global team, all working in a collaborative environment and happy to share these common values:

Curious and Clever: Smart questions spark smart solutions.

Entrepreneurial Energy: Think like an owner. Solve like a founder.

Fast with Intent: We move fast and deliver real results.

Laugh and Learn: We don’t take ourselves too seriously, just our results.




What are you waiting for?

Come for a career, stay for the fun

Job Profile Summary

The OCM Senior Analyst plays a key role in designing, leading, and delivering organisational change initiatives that enable leaders, teams, and organisations to successfully adopt changes across business processes, systems, technology, and operating models. Working as a trusted member of the Unisys Organisational Change Management (OCM) team, this role partners closely with OCM Managers, project leadership, and senior stakeholders to drive measurable adoption outcomes.

The Senior OCM Analyst brings strong hands‑on experience, sound judgment, and methodological discipline to change delivery—owning workstreams end‑to‑end, coaching junior team members, and ensuring change activities are aligned to business outcomes, risk management, and benefits realisation.

Key Responsibilities

Change Strategy & Delivery

Lead the design and execution of OCM activities aligned to project objectives, business outcomes, and stakeholder needs.

Conduct and interpret change impact, readiness, and risk assessments at individual, team, and organisational levels.

Develop and tailor integrated change strategies, including communications, engagement, training, and resistance management plans.

Proactively identify change risks and adoption barriers; design mitigation strategies to address resistance and capability gaps.

Track, analyse, and report on adoption metrics, behavioural indicators, and change effectiveness to inform decision‑making.

Contribute to sustainment planning to embed change into business‑as‑usual operations.

Translate complex transformation initiatives into clear, actionable messages for diverse audiences.

Communication & Engagement

Lead the development of high‑impact, audience‑specific communications (executive briefings, change narratives, FAQs, campaigns).

Create multimedia and digital collateral (videos, infographics, intranet content) to drive awareness and engagement.

Ensure messaging is consistent, timely, and aligned with overall program governance and brand standards.

Training & Learning Support

Lead training needs analysis and learning strategy development aligned to role‑based capability requirements.

Design and review training materials, learning journeys, and reinforcement mechanisms.

Support and co‑facilitate training sessions for frontline staff, leaders, and executives as required.

Ensure training effectiveness is measured and iterated based on feedback and adoption outcomes.

Collaboration & Project Support

Work autonomously while aligning closely with OCM Managers, Program Leads, and delivery partners.

Facilitate workshops, feedback sessions, and stakeholder briefings to gather insights and refine approaches.

Contribute to OCM practice development, standards, templates, and continuous improvement initiatives.

Support governance activities including status reporting, risk escalation, and executive updates.

Required Skills & Capabilities

Strong written, verbal, and visual communication skills in English; additional languages (e.g., German, Dutch, other European languages) are an advantage.

Proven ability to design and deliver end‑to‑end change management activities across complex initiatives.

Strong stakeholder engagement and facilitation skills, including experience working with senior leaders.

Ability to synthesise complex information and data into clear insights and practical recommendations.

Solid working knowledge of change management methodologies (e.g., Prosci / ADKAR, CMBoK) and their practical application.

Creative mindset with interest in using design tools (e.g., Adobe Creative Cloud or similar) for basic graphic or video editing.

Proficiency with Microsoft Office tools (PowerPoint, Word, Excel, SharePoint).

High level of organisation, attention to detail, and ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously.

Proactive, adaptable, and comfortable operating in fast‑paced, matrixed environments.

Preferred / Desirable Skills

Experience supporting large‑scale digital transformation, system implementations, or operating model changes.

Background in Business Process Improvement, Knowledge Management, Learning & Development, or Instructional Design.

Familiarity with reporting and dashboard tools (e.g., Power BI, ServiceNow, SharePoint analytics).

Experience leveraging AI‑assisted tools (e.g., Copilot, ChatGPT) to enhance communications, analysis, or engagement.

Exposure to regulated, government, health, or enterprise environments.

Experience contributing to OCM practice maturity, tooling, or capability uplift initiatives.

Qualifications & Experience

Bachelor’s degree in Communications, Business, Psychology, Organisational Development, Human Resources, or a related field.

2–5 years of relevant experience in Organisational Change Management, transformation, or related roles.

Prosci or equivalent change management certification (or progress toward certification) preferred.

Equivalent combination of education and experience will be considered.

Senior Pega Consultant


Location: PCS AUS, Sydney

Years of Experience: 7-10 Years

Job Summary: We are seeking a highly skilled Senior Pega Consultant with extensive experience in the Pega platform. The ideal candidate will possess a deep understanding of Pega development and implementation, and will play a crucial role in delivering high-quality consultancy services to our clients. This position requires a proactive individual who can analyze client needs and provide effective solutions using Pega technology.

Responsibilities:

Engage with clients to gather requirements and understand their business processes.
Design, develop, and implement Pega applications to meet client specifications.
Provide expert guidance on Pega best practices and methodologies.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure successful project delivery.
Conduct training sessions and workshops for client teams on Pega functionalities.
Perform system testing and troubleshooting to ensure optimal performance of Pega applications.
Stay updated with the latest Pega features and industry trends to provide innovative solutions.

Mandatory Skills:

Proven experience with Pega platform development and implementation.
Strong understanding of Pega methodologies and best practices.
Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills.
Ability to communicate effectively with clients and team members.

Preferred Skills:

Experience with Pega certification (e.g., Pega Certified Business Analyst, Pega Certified System Architect).
Familiarity with Agile methodologies.
Knowledge of integration techniques with other systems.

Qualifications:

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
7-10 years of relevant experience in Pega consultancy.
Proven track record of successful project delivery in a consultancy environment.

About Us

At Rezdy, we’re builders, doers, and difference-makers, driven by a shared mission to reshape the tours, activities, and experiences industry. Alongside our sister brands, Checkfront and Regiondo, we power more than 20,000 businesses and support over $10B in bookings globally. Our technology helps operators thrive while delivering unforgettable moments to travelers around the world.

We work in an industry built on adventure, energy, and human connection, and that same spirit fuels how we show up every day. Spanning North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific, our teams are united by bold goals, a bias for action, and an unwavering commitment to delivering for our customers.

Our success starts with people. Our teams are the engine behind everything we create. We value self-starters who take ownership, embrace challenges, and raise the bar, for themselves and those around them. We believe in creating space to grow, take risks, and make a real impact, and we celebrate those who lead with curiosity, grit, and drive.

If you’re passionate about performance, hungry to learn, and ready to help shape the future of travel—this is your kind of place. Let’s build, grow, and win together.


About the Role

We are looking for a Senior Data Engineer with 5+ years of experience to join our data team. Our data platform powers analytics for both internal business users and external customers, and plays a key role in enabling data-driven decision making across the organization.

In this role, you will primarily focus on maintaining and enhancing our existing data platform, ensuring reliability, performance, and scalability. You will also work closely with internal stakeholders to design and implement new data models in our Enterprise Data Warehouse as new reporting and analytics requirements emerge.

The platform currently supports the data and analytics needs across Rezdy, Checkfront, and Regiondo, and provides reporting capabilities to both operational teams and customers.

Our modern data stack includes Google BigQuery, Fivetran, dbt, Metabase & GoodData. This is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys working with mature data platforms, improving existing pipelines, and delivering high-quality data models that directly support business outcomes.

This is a one year fixed contract hybrid position based in Sydney, Australia. Candidates must have Australian permanent residency or citizenship to be considered.


What you will do

Maintain and support the enterprise data platform and existing data pipelines.

Ensure reliable data ingestion, transformation, and reporting across Rezdy, Checkfront, and Regiondo.

Monitor, troubleshoot, and resolve issues across the data stack to ensure data accuracy and availability.

Work closely with internal business users to understand their reporting and analytics requirements.

Design and implement new data models in BigQuery using dbt based on business needs.

Maintain and manage data ingestion pipelines.

Deliver clean, well-structured datasets that power reporting and dashboards in GoodData & Google sheets.

Optimize BigQuery queries and data models for performance and cost efficiency.

Ensure data quality, documentation, and governance across the data platform.

Contribute to improving data engineering standards, monitoring, and platform reliability.


What we are looking for

5+ years of experience in data engineering or analytics engineering roles.

Strong experience with SQL and data modeling in modern cloud data warehouses.

Hands-on experience with Google BigQuery.

Experience using dbt for transformation and data modeling.

Experience with data ingestion tools such as Fivetran or similar ELT platforms.

Experience supporting analytics platforms or BI tools.

Strong understanding of data warehousing concepts and dimensional modeling.

Experience maintaining and operating production data pipelines and platforms.

Ability to work with business stakeholders to translate requirements into scalable data models.

Strong troubleshooting and problem-solving skills.

Good documentation and communication skills.

About Us

Potentia Energy (previously Enel Green Power Australia) is a dynamic joint venture entity co-owned by two global energy leaders, Enel Green Power and INPEX, both committed to driving Australia’s energy transition and supporting the nation in achieving its net zero target. 

https://potentiaenergy.com.au/

With a proven track record and extensive expertise across the project lifecycle, Potentia Energy has established a strong presence in the Australian renewable energy sector. After acquiring a significant number of projects, our current operating portfolio currently comprises over 800MW of solar and wind capacity across Australia, with construction underway on a further approximate 200MW of capacity. This acquisition has doubled our current portfolio and has secured Potentia Energy a strong position in WA. Potentia Energy also has rights secured for a development pipeline of over 9GW across Australia and is committed to an ambitious growth agenda, targeting a significant increase to its installed capacity across wind, solar, storage and hybrid projects.

Guided by our core values – Collaboration, Authenticity, Responsibility and Excellence – we work closely with local communities, our partners and customer to contribute to a cleaner, more sustainable future.

 




The Role

Reporting to the Senior Wholesale and Commercial Manager, this role will be focused on optimising the bidding strategies of Potentia’s renewable and BESS generating assets and providing key analysis functions to support Potentia’s wholesale and retail businesses. We are looking for a proactive, motivated team player to provide crucial inputs into Potentia’s understanding of the market, wholesale risk management and renewable/battery asset operations.

This role will be key to understanding and managing Potentia’s physical and financial risks in the energy markets, protecting revenue and optimising operations.

 

Key Responsibilities

Manage bidding and dispatch service providers for Potentia Energy’s operational portfolio of solar, wind and BESS assets, including:
Being Potentia’s interface with service providers, managing the day to day relationships.
Developing execution strategies and performance reporting frameworks for monitoring and directing bidding and dispatch service providers.
Produce daily and monthly reporting (physical trading, derivative trading, financial and performance) with high attention to detail.
Provide regular analysis and reporting on energy market behaviour to support trading decisions and broader strategy, including generation and price trends and the regulatory environment.
Assist the wholesale team in the development and execution of hedging strategies.



Selection Criteria

A knowledge of Australian wholesale electricity markets (NEM and WEM) including physical and derivative trading fundamentals
Knowledge and experience of physical bidding, dispatch and Frequency Control Ancillary Services (FCAS)
Strong aptitude and experience in the visual presentation of data and analysis including slides and dashboards 
Intermediate knowledge of energy derivatives and power purchase agreements
Exceptional attention to detail
A positive attitude to operate in a fast paced and high performing team with accountabilities to global sponsors
Knowledge of relational databases (SQL) and/or the Energy One Trading system will be an advantage but not essential
Programming experience using high-level interpreted languages such as Python will be an advantage but not essential.
The right to live and work in Australia.
